title: shattering bitter norwegian cornmeal dum
description: a hearty one-pot dish originating from norway, featuring a bitter and satisfying cornmeal dum that shatters in your mouth.
instructions: heat 4 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
add 1 large onion, diced, and cook until lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
add 3 cloves of garlic, minced, and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add 2 cups of water, 1 cup of milk, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper.
bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the cornmeal has thickened.
add 1 cup of grated cheddar cheese and stir until melted and smooth.
stir in 1/2 cup of chopped fresh parsley and 1/4 cup of chopped fresh dill.
using a spoon, create 4-6 small wells in the cornmeal mixture.
crack 1 large egg into each well, then cover the pot and cook for an additional 10-12 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the cornmeal is heated through.
